round_note: FINAL (loop-until-dry). Supersedes round 2 (evidence_test prior); original preserved at evidence_test.r0.json. The 2 decidable priors are KEPT VERBATIM (superset/monotonic). The 3 UNLABELED branches are resolved honestly below: after round-2's measurement attempts returned rule_not_met / seed_starved, the campaign data itself shows none of the BUILT tools can DISCRIMINATE these branches' mechanisms, so they are marked decidable:false rather than force-labeled (G3). No relaxed-threshold re-run of any already-failed rule is proposed (forbidden).
shape_semantics: cmp,vp,vpc,naive = W,W,W,L. Every CMP-aware technique (cmplog/I2S, value_profile/Hamming, and the vpc combination) resolves the gate; only naive blocks. CMP-SOLVABLE-GATE pattern, NOT joint-necessity: either I2S or value_profile alone already resolves (cmplog=W and value_profile=W independently), so vpc does not need both. Label-source: 'any CMP technique enriches/closes the gate target; naive does not'.

hypotheses.label: A fixed-offset literal value gate (FOURCC/tag/enum/single byte) is solved by any CMP-aware fuzzer (I2S substitution or VP Hamming gradient) while naive's blind havoc cannot place the literal

params.comment: Per W/L gate offset, log2 enrichment of the corpus byte == target (W) value vs decoy (L) value, cmplog-and-value_profile vs naive. CMP-aware fuzzers should enrich the target literal at the gate offset; naive should not. Robust for WIDE literals (FOURCC/keyword, width>=4) where a multi-byte fixed-offset string is rare in a naive corpus.
params.target_value: GPOS table tag (47 50 4f 53) at table-directory offsets ~0x0c-0x0f
params.decoy_value: diffuse non-tag bytes at the same offsets in naive loser seeds

hypotheses.prediction: signed_target_enrich >= +1.0 (CMP-aware corpora over-represent the target literal bytes at the gate offset relative to naive) AND decoy_enrich <= 0 (CMP-aware does not over-represent the off-gate decoy bytes). DISCRIMINATING: a pure structural/length mechanism (not a literal-value gate) would predict signed_target_enrich ~ 0 at any fixed offset because no single byte value is privileged; an enrichment confirms the gate is a fixed-offset literal that CMP introspection targets, ruling out a structural-assembly alternative. G2: enrichment is measured over corpus byte content, independent of the resolve pattern that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: The CMP-aware corpus (value_profile Hamming gradient / I2S substitution) lands at or near the gate operand literal in a sliding-window distance sense, while naive's corpus stays far from it -- a CLOSENESS signal that survives dispersed/different-sized seeds where fixed-offset byte enrichment fails
hypotheses.novelty_vs_prior: operand_enrichment (byte_freq_ratio) requires byte-ALIGNED fixed-offset frequency of the EXACT target byte. value_distance_reached is a genuinely different measurement: per-arm MIN over sliding windows of normalized Hamming distance to the operand literal. It captures the VP Hamming-GRADIENT mechanism (h1 in every signature) that fixed-offset exact-byte frequency cannot see, and tolerates dispersed/variable-position operands. Not a relaxed-threshold re-run -- a different metric over a different descriptor.

params.comment: Per arm, MIN over the arm's seeds of (MIN over sliding windows of Hamming(window, target_operand)/width). CMP-aware arms (vp/cmp) should land close (small distance); naive should stall far. operand_literal taken per branch from the signature. Only operands with width>=3 are scored here; width<=2 operands are chance-alignment-prone (tool caveat) and NOT scored by this rule.

libxml2/6967.caveat: width<=2 chance-alignment prone; cross-server (sB). Owning server arbitrates and may honor winner_closer only if distance_gap is non-trivial; treated as a weak/backup net, not a confident label here.

hypotheses.prediction: winner_closer == true AND distance_gap >= 0.25 -- the CMP-aware arm's corpus reaches a sliding window whose Hamming distance to the operand literal is materially smaller than naive's nearest window. DISCRIMINATING: a NON-CMP mechanism (scheduling / energy / pure structural-length) predicts distance_gap ~ 0 -- naive's corpus would be just as close to the literal as the CMP arms, because nothing selects on operand-closeness. A positive gap rules out 'naive blocks for a non-value reason' and confirms a CMP-distance-climbable value gate. G2: distance is computed over raw seed bytes vs a static operand literal, independent of which fuzzer resolved.

hypotheses.label: A fixed-offset SINGLE-BYTE enum/code value gate (color_type==3) that is mechanistically the same CMP-solvable literal gate as the confirmed members, but whose 1-byte width defeats every BUILT measurement: the target byte is a common value at a single offset, so fixed-offset enrichment is too noisy to clear the threshold and a width-1 Hamming distance is chance-aligned -- non-discriminable, not a different mechanism
hypotheses.decidable_false_reason: Round-2 attached libpng/3996 to the two KEEP rules (enrichment primary, distance backup) and the campaign returned rule_not_met (1/2 rules scored, none held). The card (prompts_a/libpng_3996.analysis.json) confirms the mechanism beyond doubt -- a fixed-offset single-byte enum gate: IHDR color_type at offset 0x19 must equal 0x03 (PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE); winners carry 0x03 in 34/34 samples, naive losers carry 06/04/00. The mechanism is identical in KIND to the confirmed literal_value_gate members and to its confirmed sibling libpng/3966. BUT no BUILT tool can DISCRIMINATE it at width 1: (1) operand_enrichment / byte_freq_ratio measures fixed-offset byte frequency, and 0x03 is a ubiquitous byte value in a PNG corpus (it appears at countless non-gate offsets and inside naive seeds elsewhere), so the cmplog-vs-naive log2 enrichment of 0x03 at one offset does not reach signed_target_enrich>=1.0 -- this is exactly the registry scope_limit 'single-byte enrichment weak' realized as the rule_not_met outcome. (2) value_distance_reached is width<=2 chance-alignment-prone (registry caveat): every seed has SOME sliding window at Hamming distance 0 from a single byte 0x03 by chance, so naive's min-distance is ~0 too and distance_gap collapses to ~0 -- winner_closer cannot be established, AND a Hamming gradient is mechanistically wrong here anyway (06->04->03 is not bit-Hamming-monotone). (3) The remaining UNTRIED built tools do not fit: depth_reach is the ctx/ngram ITERATION-DEPTH label-source (wrong shape -- this branch's decisive pair is cmplog/value_profile-vs-naive, not ctx-vs-naive; using depth_reach would mislabel via a different technique and violate G2); joint_necessity / corpus_size_ratio / token_count are structural-assembly tools and there is no structure to size on a 1-byte enum. RE-USING either KEEP rule with a lower threshold to force this branch through is explicitly FORBIDDEN (relaxed-threshold re-test) and would be a forced label (G3 violation). The mechanism is real and obvious, but it is honestly NON-DISCRIMINABLE by the current BUILT tool set at width 1 -- an honest inconclusive is the correct outcome. NOTE (cross-server): libpng is on sB; this decidable:false is a TOOL-CAPABILITY judgment valid for ALL servers (it is the width-1 enum that defeats the measurement, not corpus absence), so it is not a 'corpus missing on s4' exclusion -- sB will reach the same non-discriminable verdict with the same tools. The natural fix is a future descriptor (e.g. seed_set value-set-membership / class-probability for single-byte enums that distinguishes 'corpus byte at this offset is the target value at a rate above the naive base-rate of that byte'), which the orchestrator may build; until then, decidable:false.

hypotheses.label: A non-local grammar/parse-state gate (DTD hash-triple insertion, XML prolog well-formedness chain) whose 'operand' is an abstract multi-token parser state, not a single fixed literal -- I2S repairs and VP gradient-completes dispersed structural tokens across the whole document
hypotheses.decidable_false_reason: Re-confirmed FINAL: no built measurement discriminates this sub-group's mechanism. (1) operand_literal is null in the signatures -- the gate is a parser-STATE predicate: 6405 needs a genuinely-NEW (name,name2,name3) hash triple inserted via xmlHashAddEntry3 (line 580 false on the dict path); 6690 needs wellFormed==1 preserved through a chain of dispersed '<?xml'/'DOCTYPE'/'SYSTEM' tokens up to an undeclared entity-ref. Neither has a single target byte string -- value_distance_reached has no operand to measure against, and byte_freq_ratio fails on the different-SIZED dispersed_multibyte seeds (data_reality: structural-assembly gates have different-sized winning vs blocking seeds, byte-alignment fails). (2) The tokens that DO appear (DOCTYPE, SYSTEM, <?xml) are at VARIABLE document positions and are necessary-but-not-sufficient: a corpus can contain them and still block (parse-state, not byte-presence), so even a multi-window presence count cannot prove the STATE was reached -- it is confounded with parse-success which is the resolve pattern itself (G2 violation). (3) joint_necessity / depth_reach target other shapes (assembly-depth vpc-needs-both; ctx/ngram iteration-depth) -- this is a single-technique-suffices CMP gate (cmplog=W and value_profile=W independently), not joint, and not a per-seed depth story. corpus_size_ratio / token_count measure corpus growth/structural-token counts but cannot bind a count to THIS abstract parse-state without re-introducing the parse-success confound. (4) libxml2/6405 is additionally seed_starved (W=1, L=27; need >=3 each); a rank-10 re-bisect would only restore W/L counts, not supply a measurable operand, so it stays non-discriminable even after re-bisect. The mechanism (I2S/VP repairing dispersed structural tokens) is real and likely correct, but it is honestly NON-DISCRIMINABLE with the current tool set -- honest inconclusive, not a forced label (G3). Carried verbatim from round 2 (no relaxed re-test).

data_realities_respected: operand_enrichment (KEEP hypothesis) requires an on-disk corpus AND >=3 W and >=3 L seeds; corpora exist only for curl, harfbuzz, openthread, sqlite3 on s4. It scores harfbuzz 5503/5517 (fixed-offset GPOS tag, width 4) on s4. Width-1 enum gates (libpng/3996) are NOT scored by it -- not because of corpus location but because single-byte fixed-offset enrichment is below the discrimination floor (scope_limit), see narrow_enum_value_gate_nondiscriminable.

value_distance_reached (KEEP hypothesis) reads on-disk seeds, so on-disk-corpus targets only. On s4 it scores harfbuzz 5503/5517 and sqlite3 14451 directly (width>=4). Width<=2 operands (libxml2/6967 '[', libpng/3996 0x03) are chance-alignment-prone (tool caveat); libxml2/6967 stays a cross-server weak/backup net only, libpng/3996 is NOT scored by distance (width-1 + non-monotone Hamming) and is decidable:false.

libpng/3996 width-1 enum: BOTH built value-gate tools fail by CAPABILITY (noisy single-byte enrichment + chance-aligned width-1 distance), independent of server. This is a tool-gap decidable:false valid for ALL servers, NOT a 'corpus absent on s4' exclusion. The owning server (sB) reaches the same verdict with the same tools.

structural_grammar_state_gate (libxml2/6405, 6690) is dispersed_multibyte with operand_literal=null over different-sized seeds: byte_freq_ratio byte-alignment fails (data_reality) AND there is no single operand for value_distance_reached; any token-PRESENCE count would be confounded with parse-success (the resolve pattern, G2 violation) -- honestly decidable:false, NOT force-labeled.

No seed_lineage 'I2S event' or 'VP gradient event' descriptor is proposed: I2S and value_profile are both untagged in mutation_op (I2S is a mutator with byte-identical havoc vocab; VP is a feedback, not a mutator). The only measurable signals are corpus byte content (enrichment) and corpus-to-operand distance (value_distance_reached).

notes.round3_resolution: FINAL determination of the 3 UNLABELED branches after round-2's measurement attempts: (a) libpng/3996 [round-2 rule_not_met] -> NEW hypothesis narrow_enum_value_gate_nondiscriminable, decidable:false. The card proves the mechanism is the same fixed-offset literal gate as the confirmed members (color_type==0x03 at 0x19, 34/34 winners), but width 1 defeats every built tool (0x03 too common for fixed-offset enrichment to clear +1.0; width<=2 distance chance-aligned; remaining built tools target other shapes). Forcing it through a relaxed KEEP threshold is forbidden and would be a fake label; honest inconclusive is correct (G3). (b) libxml2/6690 [round-2 rule_not_met] and (c) libxml2/6405 [seed_starved] -> stay in structural_grammar_state_gate, decidable:false (parser-state gate, null operand, parse-success confound). No relaxed re-test of any failed rule.
notes.priors_kept: BOTH decidable priors kept VERBATIM in rule + descriptor: literal_value_gate_cmp_solvable (enrichment, rule unchanged) and cmp_gradient_closes_to_operand (distance, rule unchanged). Superset/monotonic invariant honored -- the 11 confirmed branches re-validate unchanged. The only round-3 change is the explicit demotion of libpng/3996 from a round-2 cross-server member of the enrichment hypothesis (where it returned rule_not_met) to its own decidable:false narrow-enum hypothesis -- this REMOVES a forced membership, it does not alter either KEEP rule.
notes.new_mechanisms: narrow_enum_value_gate_nondiscriminable is NEW this round but decidable:false. It is NOT a different program mechanism from the confirmed literal_value_gate (it IS a fixed-offset literal gate) -- it is a TOOL-CAPABILITY boundary: the width-1 enum sub-case that no built measurement can confirm without a relaxed re-run or a new single-byte value-set-membership descriptor. Naming it separately (rather than leaving libpng/3996 force-routed to enrichment) is the honest G3 outcome and flags the precise tool gap for the orchestrator.
notes.decidable_false_subgroups: TWO decidable:false sub-groups: (1) narrow_enum_value_gate_nondiscriminable = {libpng/3996} -- width-1 enum defeats fixed-offset enrichment + width-1 Hamming distance; tool-capability gap, valid for all servers. (2) structural_grammar_state_gate = {libxml2/6405, libxml2/6690} -- null-operand parser-state gate, different-sized dispersed seeds, parse-success confound; 6405 additionally seed_starved. Both carried to fallback=inconclusive (G3).
notes.g1_discrimination: Each DECIDABLE hypothesis names the alternative it rules out: enrichment rules out a structural/length gate (~0 fixed-offset enrichment); distance rules out a non-value blocking reason (~0 distance_gap if naive blocks for scheduling/structure rather than an operand gradient). Both are falsifiable and could come out the other way. The two decidable:false hypotheses make NO measurement claim precisely because no built test could falsifiably AND discriminably distinguish their mechanism -- which is the honest reason they carry no rule.
notes.g2_independence: All DECIDABLE measurements take raw corpus/seed bytes vs a static target/operand and never consume the W/L resolve pattern that defined the shape. The structural decidable:false reason explicitly cites the G2 risk that any token-presence proxy is confounded with parse-success (= the resolve pattern), which is WHY it is left inconclusive. For libpng/3996, the width-1 distance metric would also be confounded by chance window-alignment, not by the resolve pattern, but it still fails to discriminate -- so decidable:false.
notes.g3_honesty: Round 3 is loop-until-dry: every remaining unlabeled branch is dispositioned without inventing a mechanism or relaxing a failed threshold. libpng/3996's obvious mechanism EXISTS (fixed-offset literal) but is non-measurable at width 1, so it is decidable:false with a precise tool-gap note rather than force-fit; the two libxml2 structural branches stay decidable:false. This is the correct terminal state, not a failure to label.
notes.scored_vs_inconclusive_on_s4: S4-SCORED (decidable): harfbuzz 5503/5517 (enrichment + distance), sqlite3 14451 (distance). CROSS-SERVER decidable (owning server arbitrates on its local corpus, same rules): libxml2/6967 (distance, weak width-1 net) plus lcms x5 / libpng 3901,3966 / other confirmed members. DECIDABLE:FALSE (fallback=inconclusive): libpng/3996 (width-1 enum, tool-gap), libxml2/6405, libxml2/6690 (structural parser-state). The 11 confirmed-frozen branches are untouched and re-validate under the unchanged KEEP rules.
